title: sideroxylon portoricense subsp. minutiflorum
intro: Sideroxylon portoricense subsp. minutiflorum, also known as the Puerto Rican bulletwood, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, due to its rich content of flavonoids, terpenoids, and other bioactive compounds. The leaves and roots of the plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
